title: Human Reports, Machine Output, Terminal Width, and Accessibility · Behavior Case: Terminal Width
summary: The report encodes meaning only with color, clips long words, changes columns by terminal width, mixes diagnostics with JSON stdout, and gives screen-reader users an unlabeled number grid. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the terminal width increment in accessible human and machine report system. Learners pro…
objectives: Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
Reserve stdout for requested report data and stderr for diagnostics while keeping exit status consistent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-terminal-width — Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-report-format-accessibility-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_report_format_accessibility_5(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-terminal-width-13dzba
# Competency: bookbot-terminal-width.
# Case 6974: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_terminal_width_13dzba(report_rows: int = 8, width: int = 72, diagnostics_on_stderr: bool = True, color_only_signals: int = 0, structured_alternative: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_13dzba = "13dzba-abzd31-6974"
    assert evidence_variant_13dzba
    evidence_axes_13dzba = (
        "corpus13dzba",
        "policyabzd31",
        "revision6974",
        "command13dzba6974",
        "reportabzd316974",
        "test697413dzba",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_13dzba)) == 6
    if report_rows < 1 or width < 40:
        return False, report_rows, "report-shape"
    if not diagnostics_on_stderr:
        return False, report_rows, "stream-contract"
    if color_only_signals > 0:
        return False, color_only_signals, "color-only"
    if not structured_alternative:
        return False, report_rows, "missing-alternative"
    assert width >= 40
    return True, report_rows, "accessible-report"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Terminal Width model · Theory Terminal Width, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Terminal Width precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Terminal Width model · Theory Terminal Width, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 8023 during an assistive-technology terminal review. Build deterministic Python evidence to format a reflowable report with separated diagnostics, no color-only meaning, and structured alternatives; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use a declared width or detected terminal width with minimums, wrapping, long-token policy, and snapshot tests.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Terminal Width model · Theory Terminal Width, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Hard-coded 80-column formatting is accessible on every terminal.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-terminal-width-13dzba.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-terminal-width-13dzba(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_terminal_width_13dzba\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?report_rows < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not diagnostics_on_stderr)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?color_only_signals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not structured_alternative)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
